sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the usage of binaries such as 'net', 'sc' or 'powershell' in order to stop, pause or delete critical or important Windows services such as AV, Backup, etc. As seen being used in some ransomware scripts

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Shadow Copies deletion using operating systems utilities
sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Shadow Copies Deletion Using Operating Systems Utilities
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Michael Haag, Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detect the use of "sc.exe" to change the startup type of a service to "disabled" or "demand"

values.CommandLine: C:\Windows\system32\net1 stop NetBackup BMR MTFTP Service /y

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the stopping of a Windows service
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Jakob Weinzettl, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Adversaries may stop services or processes in order to conduct Data Destruction or Data Encrypted for Impact on the data stores of services like Exchange and SQL Server.

values.CommandLine: C:\Windows\system32\net1 stop NetBackup BMR MTFTP Service /y

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects execution of Net.exe, whether suspicious or benign.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Michael Haag, Mark Woan (improvements), James Pemberton / @4A616D6573 / oscd.community (improvements)
